Product Info Sheet: Fusion Runtime Core v3.0 "Supernova"Executive SummaryFusion Runtime Core v3.0 "Supernova" is the world's first unified, heterogeneous, and distributed execution environment designed to eliminate the friction between classical computing, accelerated AI workloads, and quantum circuits. Unlike traditional runtimes that rely on generic I/O schedulers (like Tokio), Supernova implements a specialized Hyper-Ring Reactor designed for microsecond-latency task switching between CPU, GPU, QPU, and WASM contexts.Product IdentityName: Fusion Runtime Core v3.0Codename: SupernovaVersion: 3.0.0-alphaLicense: Proprietary / Enterprise Dual-LicenseCore Philosophy: "One Runtime, Every Processor, Zero Overhead."Key DifferentiatorsTokio-Free Architecture: Built on a custom FusionExecutor that treats GPU kernels and QPU shots as first-class async citizens, avoiding the "blocking thread" anti-pattern common in generic Rust async runtimes.Tiered Execution Model:Tier 1 (Native): Bare-metal performance for Rust code, CUDA kernels, and Quantum Assembly.Tier 2 (Sandboxed): WebAssembly (WASM) plugins with capability-based security for multi-tenant logic.Tier 3 (Distributed): Seamless clustering where remote nodes appear as local resources.Zero-Copy Heterogeneity: Data resides in unified memory handles (FusionPtr), allowing a WASM plugin to trigger a GPU operation on data it never physically copies.Target AudienceHigh-Frequency Trading (HFT) Firms: Requiring sub-microsecond heterogeneous dispatch.AI Research Labs: Orchestrating complex pipelines of classical preprocessing, model inference, and custom logic.Quantum Cloud Providers: Needing a unified stack to manage hybrid quantum-classical jobs.Edge Computing Platforms: Running untrusted 3rd-party WASM code alongside trusted native drivers.At a Glance: CapabilitiesFeatureDescriptionCompute EnginesCPU (x86/ARM), GPU (CUDA/ROCm), QPU (IonQ/Rigetti adapters)Plugin SystemHot-swappable WASM with strict resource quotas (Fuel, RAM)NetworkingCustom RDMA-aware transport (No generic TCP overhead)SecurityCapability-based Sandbox (FileSystem, Network, Device Access)
